description: Use government spending and taxation as a fiscal policy instrument — including discretionary and automatic stabilisers — to influence aggregate demand, employment, and inflation

fields.name: change_amount
fields.label: Proposed change in currency units or percent of GDP

objectives: Stabilise aggregate demand and smooth the business cycle
Fund public goods and provide safety nets

fiscal_stance.expansionary: Higher spending and/or lower taxes — stimulates AD
fiscal_stance.contractionary: Lower spending and/or higher taxes — restrains AD
fiscal_stance.neutral: Structural deficit unchanged after adjusting for cycle

tools.direct_taxes: Income, corporate, capital gains, payroll — slow to adjust
tools.indirect_taxes: VAT, excise duties — quick to adjust

automatic_stabilisers.definition: Fiscal features that moderate cycles without new legislation
automatic_stabilisers.examples: Progressive income tax, unemployment insurance, means-tested benefits
automatic_stabilisers.mechanism: Rise in recessions, fall in expansions — dampens AD swings

discretionary_vs_automatic.discretionary: Requires new legislation; subject to lags
discretionary_vs_automatic.automatic: Kicks in with the cycle; no legislative delay

applications.equity_sector_bets: Infrastructure spending lifts industrials and materials
applications.credit_positioning: Fiscal expansion widens supply of sovereigns -> spreads/curve
applications.currency: Large fiscal deficits can weaken currency via rising rates and supply

validation.valid_tool: policy_tool in {government_spending, direct_tax, indirect_tax, transfer_payment, automatic_stabiliser}

evaluate_fiscal_action.description: Assess a proposed fiscal policy change

errors.message: policy_tool must be one of government_spending, direct_tax, indirect_tax, transfer_payment, automatic_stabiliser

anti_patterns.rule: Do not treat all tax cuts as equally stimulative
anti_patterns.why: Tax cuts to high-income households boost savings more than consumption; lower-income transfers have much higher marginal propensity to spend

anti_patterns.rule: Do not ignore the fiscal multiplier's dependence on cycle phase
anti_patterns.why: Multipliers are larger at the zero lower bound and in recessions than in full-employment expansions

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use current deficit levels as a sufficient measure of fiscal stance
anti_patterns.why: Headline deficits include cyclical components; the structural or cyclically adjusted deficit is the correct stance measure

anti_patterns.rule: Do not assume fiscal stimulus is always expansionary in the long run
anti_patterns.why: Expected tax hikes or crowding-out of private investment can offset short-run stimulus (Ricardian equivalence)

advantages_disadvantages.direct_tax_adv: Progressive; collects from capacity to pay
advantages_disadvantages.spending_adv: Can target specific regions or sectors
